dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Word of advice about good Lady Aphrodite? Don't get distracted. She speaks of love and beauty, though she's no stranger to conflict. Perhaps my power can offset her own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Between us, I have never seen Ares so pleased as he has been recently. And I can probably imagine why. This war that we've been waging... it's a real piece of work, even for him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: At least my good half-brother Ares finally made an appearance. I was beginning to wonder if he'd ever show up! Though, he might have wondered that about me in the past...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: If Artemis was terse or impolite with you, don't take it personally, please. It's how she is with everyone. Always been the solitary sort, and we never know where she is when we need her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: My half-sister Athena knew your brother well, and sends her regards. She's preoccupied with our defense, leading our front-line forces and all that! She regrets that she can't aid you thus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Couldn't help but notice there's no bow in your arsenal! Artemis and I, we figure why sully our hands when we can settle any ugly business from afar... although your magick may suffice!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: That Chronos is up to something, but not even Athena knows what. His assaults on Olympus always fail, even if they've kept us occupied. What's his next move...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: To think we'd wage war against our own grandfather. Not that we should give Chronos that sort of credit. He's a disgrace to our line; a hateful Titan, not a god. Not family.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I rather hoped old Chronos and his ilk would have seen the light by now, but alas. We'll just have to keep shining it in their faces for a while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You've struck a solid blow against the Titan's forces, but they've started to regroup. Still, these types of victories must shake them to the core. Let's drive them back!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: They're saying you got Chronos, didn't you, sunshine? Last night at least, but that's the deathless Titans for you! Never staying down for long. Though you've something in mind for that, my sister says...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: So you were born and raised in some secluded grove down there? No luxuries, mortal worshipers... so much living to catch up on once this ugly matter is resolved.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: When Artemis first told me about you, I couldn't help but think of Demeter. She couldn't be there for your mother when Chronos struck, but I know she'll do everything she can for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You can count on most of us, but I can't speak for Dionysus; don't even know where he is! I like a feast as much as anybody at the proper time, but the god of wine thinks the proper time is all the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Still no sign of Dionysus. I'd be worried if it wasn't him, but he gets like this. Sometimes I cannot believe we're related. I want a bit of order around here, he wants the opposite.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You must be in the city of Ephyra to the west, a staging ground for our enemies... with the Underworld gates thrown open, Chronos can replenish his forces faster than we can kill them off.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Wonder what there is to do for leisure down in Erebus, when one isn't rigorously training to vanquish hateful Titans and such. There must be something...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: We've confirmed sightings of that Eris buzzing about. She's from your realm not ours, so you may be more privy to how come she's showed up uninvited! Though, that tends to be her style. Somebody needs to shoo her away, already...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't like getting swept up in our family's petty feuds, especially with older generations that apparently have nothing better to do. But you and I couldn't exactly sit this one out, could we?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: No wonder Chronos hates us, right? Admittedly we've got some prickly personalities here, but for all our quirks, we're family; and we stick together. Something Titans wouldn't understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I didn't think matters could get much more precarious for us, but if the Fates are truly gone, then... their prophecies about the future may not come to pass. They foretold our family's eternal rule over the world... what if they were wrong?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Even if you managed to make Chronos see the light, the Three Fates are still missing. And we're supposed to believe he doesn't know where they are? This couldn't possibly be what they wanted or foretold...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You're in the Mourning Fields. I wonder does the Hyacinth grow there... the flower of a gentle prince, long gone. Once the Underworld reopens, perhaps I could go seek him out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, sunshine, it's just the two of us again, now that you slaughtered every... what exactly do you call those? Well, no matter, here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I never was particularly close with your father, but I guess neither were you. Always struck me as a gloomy sort, though he did seem to care a lot about his own. He'll get his justice yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Our world's been through worse times than this, Cousin! So I am told at least, I've not seen anything this bad. Still, it's nothing we can't handle, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You trained under Hecate, the Witch of the Crossroads? She's well respected here, though we've not seen her in a while, of course. I often speak ill of Titans lately but I'm reminded that they're not all bad.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: It's complicated who owns what up here. You know Helios the Sun? He's back to tramping across the sky in his chariot like nothing's changed, but he'd be lost without my light! Eugh, Titans. What can you do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Hephaestus has been hard at work on our defenses, so I'm pleased you've heard from him. Don't take his surly manner the wrong way! He doesn't get out much, certainly not these days.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, you've met my stepmother, have you? I'm fortunate to feel loved, but Hera, well, I'm not her favorite. Long as no one leads her to believe you're Zeus's child, you will be just fine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You got there faster than expected, which might mean you got a bit of a boost from Hermes! He's swift for sure, though I can never get a straight answer out of him about anything...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes I think Hermes must still be running about in the Underworld like he used to, as lately I can't seem to find him anywhere. He's been so cagey with me, too! Some messenger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey you got old Hestia to crawl out of the woodwork! She normally wants nothing to do with any of us, though this business with Chronos got her all worked up. Well, good!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: It's often said there's nothing new under the sun, though I've not seen a weapon quite like what you're wielding there! The legendary Arms of Night, Hephaestus says. Impressive stuff!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I always wished to see the House of Hades, though your father and mother have a royal guard I may have sent to their realm prematurely. It'd make things rather awkward if we ran into each other there...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: See, sunshine? Whenever you head out with the Harmonic Photon that you got from me, I won't be far behind. And you'll get a warm fuzzy feeling for good measure!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: That Photon I gave you has seen plenty of good use by now, hasn't it? I know you don't always need a light in the darkness like so many others, so I'm pleased it could help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: What sort of light can I provide for you today? I've got hard light, soft light, blinding light, last thing a mortal ever sees light... you name it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You ever wonder what light really is, Cousin? Can't grasp it in your hand, doesn't weigh a thing, yet it's all around us! A real mystery... I'm waiting for the mortals to figure it out!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You're more of a night person, aren't you, sunshine? Accustomed to the dark. Me, I'm more the broad daylight type, but hey, to each their own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know how you managed all this time without so much as a ray of light down there. Different worlds, I guess, but sometimes good things happen when such worlds collide.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: When Artemis first told me your tale, I figured it was just one of her pranks. Taking on the Titan yourself, come on. But it did sound like something a vengeful child of Hades might attempt...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: What is that fancy staff you've got, with a serpent coiled at the top? How strange, it's... reminiscent of a vision that I've had... about a masterwork that can do no harm...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Mortals look to us for guidance and inspiration. They must be horrified by what they're seeing of late! Half of them seem to be against us now. Let's hope it's the other half that's left once all this is over.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: For such a fearful lot, mortals sure love killing each other! There was this war they had a while back that even had us gods at one another's throats. I tried to settle it with plague and arrows and such, but no! Father wanted it to play out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Lately I'm reminded just how frail mortals are... I never let myself get attached anymore. They can be so much like us, even beautiful, but there's all heartbreak watching them die. Their deaths aren't even the end, but they sure act like it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Artemis says you're rather musically inclined! Runs in the family, even your parents have an ear for it what with Orpheus as their court musician. He's the best I ever taught!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Where in blazes are you now, sunshine? It's like you're at the bottom of the sea. Wherever it is, it's awful dark, and as you know, I've always got the cure for that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: We'll keep holding it together on this mountaintop, just as long as you keep holding it together... wherever it is you happen to be! Take care, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait, you're right on Olympus now, sunshine! So close to us, though I am painfully aware the worst is yet to come as you press on. Fortunately, I have the best for you right here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I can't believe we've let those Satyrs overrun our mountainside... leave it to Dionysus to try and make friends with them, but I've had no such fortune, believe me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know what it is exactly but something about this night made me flash back to when first you contacted me in those very woods. Well, either strange forces are at work or just my imagination, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You're just outside of the Ephyra gates, aren't you? That city is beyond salvation; I know they used to worship your mother and father there, but still... if it were up to me alone, I'd sweep the place away and start anew.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: There's something exceedingly odd about tonight. Haven't we already had an evening just like this, down to the smallest detail? Your magick at work perhaps... as long as it isn't Chronos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait how exactly did you even get there, sunshine? Last I checked you were traversing someplace else entirely! But then I had a hunch to check again, and sure enough...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I know you're not one to complain, but it couldn't have been easy for you being at the Palace here at the break of Dawn... probably much more light than you're used to. We're hardly used to it either now thanks to all of Typhon's storms.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I like being the god of many things. There's light, and poetry, music, healing... so many passions, and all the time in the world. Why settle for just blacksmithing or what-have-you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: The sightless Cyclops Polyphemus bars your passage, does he now? Don't know what good my light will do against the likes of him, though perhaps I can help get you there in one piece.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Beneath our uncle Poseidon's affable veneer is a ferocity I've learned to carefully avoid. If he's backed you with his ocean-strength, you're better off. Go make the most of it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Prometheus himself, leading the assault against us... one of our sole allies amongst Titankind, become a bitter foe. All because of how we treated him! Or because... he knows which side shall be victorious when all is said and done...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Didn't think we'd let Prometheus continue his assault like this. Some partnership we've formed, all so you can re-create the path you took to Typhon, to then face the monster's remnants on the summit as before...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: If Prometheus truly had the foresight that everything would happen as it did... why bar your path and fight so fiercely, only to be defeated again and again...? Unless the dawning of a new age wouldn't come about in any other way...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Bit embarrassed to admit that I'm the god of prophecy, among everything else. I ought to have seen this Titan business coming! But I didn't... and old Chronos caught us out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: As god of prophecies, I'm certain that we're going to succeed. Exactly how, though, I can't say for sure. That's prophecies for you! Blasted frustrating things most of the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: When I first reached out to you many nights ago, I figured, hey, another cousin! I like those! But the truth is I never expected you to make as great a difference as you have. My hope is I've done my part to make it possible.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Aphrodite's been quite excited for you... she has a way of knowing everybody's supposedly-private lives, but if she's blessing your relationships, you're better off. Some of mine certainly could have used it...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: So you get to pick your poison between going after Chronos in the Underworld, or coming to our rescue here! Truth is there's been some dispute as to which is the brighter idea. But, you must know what you're doing better than we.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You seem fairer than ever, Cousin. Awash in moonlight...  you're working with Selene, aren't you? And here I thought she'd be too busy galloping across the sky to back us up...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: The light of day can be a bit harsh, so it's fortunate the Moon offers such a pleasant alternative. Selene's shine may be more to your liking, if you can even see her from there...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Such a dark and oppressive night this is shaping up to be... even if I could illuminate all your foes waiting in ambush, I'm not sure you'd want to see them. Be very careful out there...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: My sister Artemis once also trained with Hecate, you know! Trained in what, however, I have no idea. I still remember how they'd howl at night like a pack of wolves, heh. Bit unnerving, honestly!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: My word, you reached the surface, didn't you, sunshine? I thought your birthright meant you couldn't live up here...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: The surface used to be more pleasant. But ever since the siege, Uncle Poseidon has been going all out with quakes and cataclysms in response. Stirring up the land like it's his mixing bowl.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Cousin, weren't you headed to Olympus last I checked? Must have been waylaid by the Titan's thugs. Well, don't give up! Although I guess you wouldn't be there if you did.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, we've got a situation starting to unfold. A big crevasse opened up at the base of the mountain, and something's in there... something big. And it's breathing... suppose it's friendly...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I caught merely a glimpse, though that was poetry in motion the way you handled yourself against that lot. Talent runs in the family! Some of us just wound up with a bigger share.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: How fares your voyage here, sunshine? If only we could whisk you over with more-godlike means, but the skies are as treacherous as the waters... as I suspect you've noticed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Do bide your time, sunshine. The Titan thinks he has us pinned, but we have the situation here under control! Just locked in a bit of a stalemate is all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Augh, this blasted war. Leaves little time for music or for poetry or any of life's joys. Though, it's brought the family closer together, I guess!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You were a little easier to spot with twin torches in hand... though yours don't seem to cast the customary kind of light! Truly the more I learn of you, the more questions I have!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Ever wondered just how gargantuan is Typhon, the Father of All Monsters, truly? Well, you need but pay a visit here and you'll find out! Till then, erm, we'll... just have to fend him off!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Judging by all his vengeful roars, Typhon must hate my father almost as much as Chronos does. But are they truly working together? A monster such as that couldn't possibly be taking orders from a Titan, or anybody.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Chronos must have thought nothing could stop him if he unleashed Typhon, but he thought wrong. Let's throw everything we've got at him and finish this, sunshine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: So we're now working to prevent even the possibility that Typhon could have overwhelmed us here? And the Titan's forces are somehow part of this new plan. Well, if you're in, I'm in. Should make my evenings more exciting for a bit!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I still can't entirely believe you got him, sunshine! Typhon should have taken the combined might of our entire family, and in a way he did, it's just... the rest of us were busy fighting back his swarms. He's really got us stretched thin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: If Typhon keeps coming back, eventually there may be no mountain here left to climb. I know my father and Queen Hera have a plan in case things could not get more dire, but I'm more optimistic for whichever plan you have in mind...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: We should be able to hold back Typhon for a while longer, especially now that you've shown us a thing or two about how it's done, sunshine. You're going after Chronos now, aren't you? Well ridding us of him certainly wouldn't hurt.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Even though your mother and father are back, you can't just stroll down to the bottom of the Underworld, can you? Finding remnants of Chronos means re-creating the conditions in which you faced him before, something like that...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: So you're a witch, are you? Interesting craft. Your magick-making words possess a certain rhythm and power. Poetry is much the same! Except its magick affects only the heart.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: So pleased you finally got to meet your brother by the way! Me, I have more than enough of those, but if you grew up as an only child, you could probably use one. And it's never too late to make up for lost time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: My father Zeus already got to you, did he? Blessed you, I should say! He's a god of many inclinations. But when our rule is threatened, or even insulted, no one fights back more fiercely.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps you knew this Boon was going to be mine, for how can my light be contained, even in a heavy parcel? It must have been glowing a little at least, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Normally my light makes it easy to spot whoever needs a blessing or a smiting, but you always seem to be in shadow! Fortunately, Charon seems to know how to get a hold of you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's show them a dazzling display they won't soon forget.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Won't take but a moment here and you'll be positively radiant, you'll see.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd rather we handled all this in the light of day, but whatever works!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Brighter days are ahead, just have to get through this rough patch.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: What can we do but try and make these dark days a bit brighter, Cousin?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You're going to put on a brilliant performance tonight, Cousin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Peace will come again eventually, but for now, we have a reputation to uphold.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: From all of us upon Olympus, we are grateful for your ongoing support.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Must be something I can do to brighten up your evening there a bit?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Go on, sunshine, and may my light help guide you where you need to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't have a nice day out in the sun, well here's the next best thing!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You're brilliant without my light, though here's a little just in case.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Much as I'd rather offer poetry and music, right now these shall have to do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Where there's light, there's hope, sunshine, so get set for some of each!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, this is going to be a sensational night, I can already tell!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Your foes won't so much as touch you once we dazzle them a bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd cast a plague upon our enemies but think you'll do the trick.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Tread lightly down there, and remember, we're with you all the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Remember, don't go heading down into the dark without a light.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I confess I'm rather enjoying this particular song and dance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: My light normally can't reach the depths of the Underworld, but you can change all that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: We're all in this together till the end, sunshine! Whenever that is going to be, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't like getting swept up in family affairs, but I couldn't exactly sit this one out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: As god of prophecies, I'm certain that we're going to succeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Talent runs in the family! Some of us just wound up with a bigger share.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Look at you, strutting through that Underworld as though you own the place!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know how you managed all this time without so much as a ray of light down there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: What sort of light can I provide for you today?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know about you, but I've got a good feeling about how this is going to go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You were a little easier to spot with twin torches in hand!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Such a dark and oppressive night this is shaping up to be...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Before you go charging off to battle and glory once more, please, allow me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: May this blessing be of benefit every step of the way tonight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: For all the Boons we've offered, you've given us quite the blessing in exchange.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I like being the god of many things... so many passions, and all the time in the world!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Just a spell, it's just another spell. Ten words... In the name of Hades! Olympus, I accept this message!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Greetings from Olympus and well met, there, sunshine! Look, we can't get to the Titan, but if you truly mean to stop him, then the least I can do is light your way down.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Cousin Apollo! I am grateful for all your warmth, benevolence, and aid! Please, accept this gift!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: An offering, for me? What a delightful surprise, and I haven't had a lot of those of late. Know what, I've something for you, too!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wonder, does this bottle's brilliant sheen come from the god of light, himself? Perhaps to him it should return, right now!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Such a thoughtful gift! It's gracious of you to think of me like this, and during such a time. Though there's no need to go out of your way!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Brilliant Apollo, your many talents must be the envy of all the land, and I am honored that you're sharing them with me. Here, this is for you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Come now, you truly shouldn't have. Such flattery's entirely unnecessary, I assure you! Although it's perfectly welcome!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They say this Nectar shines as brightly as the sun, Cousin. May it live up to such a promise, especially now that it's yours! I'm beyond fortunate to have you as a friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you're too kind. You didn't have to get me this! I never knew you Underworld gods were so damn courteous. You know our customs better than we do!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My glorious cousin! It was you who first reached out to me of all those on Olympus. I've come this far because of you, and no matter what happens, I'll never forget the kindness you showed me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on, the pleasure is mine! It's not all sunshine on Olympus recently. But then I hear from you, and I'm left feeling everything will be all right. Don't quite know how that happened, but I'm very grateful that we met.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Dear Cousin, you must have Nectar in no short supply. But I know that the depth of the sentiment is what distinguishes one bottle from the next. So please accept this one; it should be good.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm an Olympian; I'm used to glad-handers and sycophants. Everybody wants our favor, right? And I can smile and nod better than anyone, even Father. Yet with you... well, if only we had more chances to talk than trade in courtesies...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Apollo, my cousin, my friend! This Ambrosia reminds me of the moment I first saw your father's palace and heard your wondrous song. Thank you for letting me see our world in a new light, and for always believing in me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Believing in you couldn't have been easier, Lady Melinoë, even without this Ambrosia dancing with moon beams from the Crossroads. I feel truly blessed to have met you, and that my sister found you when she did. You're the greatest boon our family has received in a long, long time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, sunshine... what do you say we cut the song and dance and set you up with something positively brilliant this time. See here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: There's no one god of vengeance on Olympus, because we all share that responsibility at times. Go on, take back what's yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: It's fortunate the Underworld Boatman's still plying his trade even during a downturn such as this! I trust his prices have gotten better, at least? Or, perhaps worse...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: We have to reach you however we can, and Charon's been more than keen to oblige. Bit of an odd fellow, but then, aren't we all?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: That Boatman's something of a coin collector, isn't he? Well, once we're finished with old Chronos, all those Gold Crowns of his will be extremely rare collector's items!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: We started working with Charon back when we had to figure out a system to transport all the mortal dead down to your father's realm. Must be more pleasant hauling messages from us!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You seem a bit out of sorts, sunshine. I'm supposed to be the god of healing, though even I can't aid you all that much in your present state. But let me see what I can do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh, you seem unwell, Cousin. Either you're pushing yourself too hard, or your foes landed a few too many hits. Perhaps both. Take care, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm a bit of a physician, sunshine, and my professional opinion is your vital signs are not the best right now. Although perhaps we can still get you back in shape...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh, looks like I've run a little late, as you most likely could have used assistance from me prior to whatever gave you such a thorough drubbing. Try to hold on, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, enough of this. Let's turn our combined might back in the same direction, and move on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: That got a bit ugly, didn't it? How about we both stay focused on the task at hand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Forgive my little outburst there, would you? Just a bit of bad temper. Runs in the family.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: A momentary flash of anger was all! We gods are easily hurt, for a bunch of immortals.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You were just messing with me back there, right? I normally can take a prank just fine, but we've all been a bit on edge.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: No use keeping anger bottled up. We're gods! We control everything but our emotions, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Relationships grow stronger through adversity. So ours must have grown a little stronger there!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, sorry about that, sunshine. When my wrath flares up, probably best to not be anywhere in the vicinity.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, so much for that lot! May my blessing serve you better than them. You earned it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I let frustrations get the best of me, but that entertaining spectacle shook me out of it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: The Fates are back, all three of them, sunshine! Such a relief to feel their prophecies again. They even now foretell the dawning of a new and different age. Plenty of time to prepare... though let's make the most of it, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: How did there come to be so much deceit in this world, Cousin? Believe me, we've needed a god of truth around here sometimes. Happy to do it and hope it catches on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Hope you don't mind I call you sunshine, by the way! I mean it when I say it. I know you come from underneath the earth, but there's this brilliance about you. Your mother's side I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: There anybody special in your life? A princess like yourself must have suitors of all sorts. I've had my share, though I'm not the marrying type, and besides, I don't think Hera would oblige...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: For all the Boons we've offered, you've given us quite the blessing in exchange. Olympus has powerful allies... well, you and Heracles, at least! But you have this spark so many others don't.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: It was a joy and honor to accompany you to the Crossroads on Selene's silver chariot, Cousin. The ballad Artemis and I performed... that's the first we jointly composed. We don't always get along but you inspired us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm impressed our fathers reached an agreement about Chronos, and certainly did not expect they'd send him to the Isles of the Blessed! Best spot in all the Underworld, so I hear. May he keep it that way!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: The song Artemis and I composed, we call it Time Belongs to Us. Came up with it when our hope of overcoming Chronos was starting to slip, but my knack for prophecy told me we had to hold on...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You wound me, Cousin. After everything we've been through together, you'd brush me off so easily?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Augh! Now really isn't the time for petty squabbles, but I'm afraid you leave me with no choice...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Not your first choice?! Perhaps you haven't heard, so I'll enlighten you: My generosity is not unlimited.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I have few weak points, but my pride is one. You got me where it hurts, so now I've got to get you back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Was it something I said or did? Or perhaps you don't respect my power. Even when it's turned against you thus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I go out of my way to offer you my strength, and you refuse? That's not something any self-respecting god can abide.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on, sunshine, why the snub? You should know that when my feelings get hurt, it's never the only thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I have a reputation to live up to. I can't just let you brush me off like some pathetic commoner with nothing but a prayer!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps someday we'll all be able to resolve our differences more sensibly. But we're the gods, and we're a family, damn it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Beneath this sunny disposition, I have something of a vengeful streak, Cousin. Though I know actions speak louder than words.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Just getting started for the evening, are you, sunshine? Don't know about you, but I've got a good feeling about how this is going to go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: The night is young, and I found you already. You don't suppose some of my sister's tracking skill is starting to rub off on me, do you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Before you go charging off to battle and glory once more, please, allow me! And may this blessing be of benefit every step of the way tonight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Thought you could use this extra bit of preparation before heading out again in earnest, Cousin! A bit of extra reassurance that you'll make it all the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Look at you, strutting through that Underworld as though you own the place! Although perhaps you do? Not sure how it works with the succession plans down there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: The other evening, we lost contact with you in the deepest level of the Underworld. Soon after, though, our enemies flew into disarray, at least for a while. Your doing wasn't it? Fine work!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You found a harmful trace of Chronos, now a trace of Typhon's next? Glad there's no real sign of them from where we sit... besides their scattered forces and the devastation that they caused, but that can be cleared up!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I, erm, lost my train of thought, Cousin, for Aphrodite here, she doesn't seem to understand the meaning of a private conversation. Lady, would you care to join us, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: AphroditeUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Oh, dear Apollo, yes, I always care to join your conversations, especially the private ones! And is there any who could blame me? Love and sunshine go together, after all!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Ares and I were just chatting about how we seem to get on best when we're on the same side of a war. So, Brother, is this all an elaborate ploy to bring us closer together?

dialogueLines.speaker: AresUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: I think it's merely that a proper war brings out the best in all of us; even in you, Apollo, and in me. The rigors of conflict forge unbreakable bonds! And make for powerful blessings.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: All of us here want you to succeed, Cousin, though none so much as Lady Demeter... I'm reminded that you are her grandchild, after all. Erm, wait, she wants to speak to you herself!

dialogueLines.speaker: DemeterUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for your advocacy services, Apollo. Should I have need of them again, I shall apprise you. For now, my granddaughter requires our abilities, not honeyed words.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, Cousin, one thing you and Hephaestus have in common is you don't seem to get a lot of sun! I often try to coax him from his cave, and while I usually fail, not this time!

dialogueLines.speaker: HephaestusUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Come off it, mate, before the witchie catches on to your routine! Now make yourself useful over here, lest I stick this hammer right where you don't get a lot of sun!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: I know you were expecting me, sunshine, but my glorious stepmother the Queen would like a word! This instant... so I'd better step aside, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: HeraUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: You see, my dear? There are yet those upon Olympus who show their Queen the proper deference. Seek not to earn the love of your subjects; make them a bit afraid, instead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Great Hestia has joined us here, Cousin! Returned from all her journeys 'cross the world, to lend her wisdom in our time of need. Where glows her hearth-flame, you shall also find my light. And together, we can, oh—!

dialogueLines.speaker: HestiaUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Oh shut it, Golden Boy, we don't have time for pageantry! Instead, how about we show your wee cousin there how we can light up the night? Won't do for her to wander in the dark!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Cousin, our uncle and I were just debating what to do about mortals who turned their backs on us... I think a year or so of plague should settle up the score! But, Lord Poseidon here thinks it's more important to go big...

dialogueLines.speaker: PoseidonUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Why would you not go big, given the choice, my delicate nephew? I'll shake the earth so strongly it shall swallow all those nasty mortals up, and deliver them straight down into the arms of your comely cousin there!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, greetings, honored Cousin! I'm here now with none other than the bringer of thunder and lightning, magnanimous King of Olympus, your great Lord Uncle and my illustrious father, Zeus!

dialogueLines.speaker: ZeusUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Oh, haha, my niece and I are well past such a level of formality, my son! Though while we have the pleasure of her company, what say we show her how we put the light into lightning?
